mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales indicates synchronized strength across broad economic output and consumer spending, reinforcing earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term marginal predictive contribution is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above its six-month trend signals recovering manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative in >50% of post-crossing periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline during disinflationary phases.

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and raises discount rates, systematically producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns exceed 60% of triggered instances, or the combined inflation-rate regime shows no statistically significant drawdown relative to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate earnings trajectories and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth, or the series exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in housing starts exceeding 10% indicates collapsing construction demand and tightening credit conditions, prompting immediate equity market sell-offs and negative returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered events, or the housing signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of government stimulus.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial output and elevated unemployment confirms a broad-based economic slowdown, eroding corporate revenue forecasts and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise with Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.
